title: Team
text:
OptoLAB is led by Prof. Luigi Rovati and brings together expertise in measurement science, biomedical engineering, vision science, electronics and optical instrumentation.




Luigi Rovati
Full Professor · Laboratory Head
Measurement science and optoelectronic instrumentation for biomedical and ophthalmic applications, with particular attention to weak optical signals and metrological characterization.

luigi.rovati@unimore.it

UniFind profile · ResearchGate





Stefano Cattini
Associate Professor
Electronic measurements, sensor systems and measurement methods for industrial, automotive and biomedical applications.

stefano.cattini@unimore.it

UniFind profile · ResearchGate · ORCID





Giovanni Gibertoni
Assistant Professor · Bioengineering
Biomedical optical instrumentation, ophthalmic imaging, image-quality assessment and computational methods for ocular measurements.

giovanni.gibertoni@unimore.it

UniFind profile · ResearchGate · GitHub





Davide Cassanelli
Researcher · Automotive
Optical measurement systems and sensor characterization, including LiDAR safety and portable fluorescence instrumentation.

davide.cassanelli@unimore.it

UniFind profile · ResearchGate





Agostino Gibaldi
Assistant Professor · Bioengineering
Vision science, binocular vision, eye tracking, wearable systems and computational models of visual behaviour.

agostino.gibaldi@unimore.it

UniFind profile · ResearchGate





Daniele Goldoni
Researcher · Biomedical
Biomedical measurement systems, electronics, signal processing and sensor-based methods for biological and environmental analysis.

daniele.goldoni@unimore.it

UniFind UNIMORE · ResearchGate · Google Scholar





Alberto Besozzi
PhD Student
Doctoral researcher working on compact optical sensors, flexible calibration and wearable measurement systems under the supervision of Agostino Gibaldi and Luigi Rovati.

alberto.besozzi@unimore.it

UniFind profile · PhD programme · Recent publication

title: Research
text: OptoLAB designs, develops and characterizes optical and optoelectronic measurement systems for biomedical and ophthalmic applications. The laboratory combines experimental prototyping with modelling, simulation and signal processing across the complete measurement chain.

title: Research
text:
Ophthalmic instrumentation
We develop methods and research instruments for eye diagnostics and imaging, including eye-position monitoring, anterior-segment assessment and automated analysis of ocular images.

title: Research
text:
Biomedical optical imaging
We study imaging and fluorescence-measurement systems in which optical architecture, acquisition strategy and computation are designed together around a biomedical or environmental measurement question.

title: Research
text:
Wearable optical sensing
This area focuses on compact instruments for monitoring the personal visual environment and light exposure, integrating optical sensing, embedded electronics and context-aware data analysis.

title: Research
text:
Optoelectronic instrumentation
We develop experimental measurement chains that connect light sources, detectors, analog and digital electronics, control software and data interpretation, with attention to weak-signal acquisition and metrological characterization.

title: Research
text:
Embedded acquisition systems
Embedded platforms support synchronized acquisition, experimental control, communication and portable instrument prototypes. Readable protocols and inspectable software are part of the research and teaching workflow.

title: Research
text:
Colorimetry and spectral sensing
We investigate colorimetric, photometric, radiometric and multispectral measurement approaches, including flexible calibration methods for compact, low-cost multi-channel sensors.

title: Research
text:
Computational imaging and artificial intelligence
We develop computational approaches for reconstruction, quality assessment, feature extraction and interpretation of optical data, including machine-learning methods for ophthalmic images and curated research datasets.

title: Research
text:

title: Wearable Visual-Environment Monitoring
text: Ongoing research

title: Wearable Visual-Environment Monitoring
text:
Research context
The visual environment—including illumination and near-work behaviour—is an important research dimension in the study of myopia.

title: Wearable Visual-Environment Monitoring
text:
Research approach
OptoLAB is exploring wearable, multi-sensor instruments that combine ambient-light measurement, embedded acquisition and visual-context analysis. The work brings together optical sensing, vision science, prototyping and signal interpretation.

title: Wearable Visual-Environment Monitoring
text:
Public overview
The laboratory’s public ResearchGate page includes the 2025 conference contribution A Wearable Device for Comprehensive Monitoring of the Visual Environment in Myopia Research.
View the OptoLAB ResearchGate profile

title: Ophthalmic Measurement Support
text: Research direction

title: Ophthalmic Measurement Support
text:
Research context
The quality of an ophthalmic measurement depends not only on the instrument, but also on eye position and image quality during acquisition.

title: Ophthalmic Measurement Support
text:
Current work
OptoLAB investigates real-time eye-position monitoring and automated ocular-image analysis. Public outputs include the OptoCheck approach for monitoring eye position during ophthalmic examinations, image classification for ophthalmic measurement systems and the PopEYE infrared ocular-image dataset.

title: Ophthalmic Measurement Support
text:
Selected outputs

OptoCheck institutional record
Vision-based eye image classification
PopEYE dataset record
Educational embedded-camera resource

title: OptoLAB
text:
University of Modena and Reggio Emilia




Optical technologies for biomedical imaging, sensing and ophthalmic instrumentation.


Led by Prof. Luigi Rovati, OptoLAB brings together optics, electronics, embedded systems and computation to develop and characterize optical and optoelectronic measurement systems for biomedical and ophthalmic applications.


Explore our research Contact the lab







Abstract illustration of spectral light beams crossing optical elements and converging on a sensor

title: OptoLAB
text:
Across optics, instruments and computation
We investigate measurement chains as connected systems: optical interaction, sensor response, electronic acquisition and computational interpretation.

title: OptoLAB
section: From measurement idea to validated method
text:
From measurement idea to validated method
Recent work spans calibrated compact sensors, ophthalmic measurement support and wearable monitoring of the visual environment.

title: OptoLAB
text:
Building the complete measurement chain
Our work spans the interfaces between light, hardware and information, from optomechanical prototyping and sensor characterization to embedded acquisition and computational analysis.

title: Flexible Calibration of Multi-Channel Sensors
text: Published research · 2026

title: Flexible Calibration of Multi-Channel Sensors
text:
Research context
Compact multi-channel sensors can support photometric and radiometric measurements when their spectral response and calibration chain are treated explicitly.

title: Flexible Calibration of Multi-Channel Sensors
text:
Published work
The work presents a flexible calibration method that relates multi-channel measurements to photometric and radiometric quantities. It was developed by Alberto Besozzi, Giovanni Gibertoni, Luigi Rovati and Agostino Gibaldi and published in Acta IMEKO.

title: Flexible Calibration of Multi-Channel Sensors
text:
Outputs

title: Software & Resources
text: The following cards are based only on public repository descriptions and README files retrieved from the OPTOLAB-UNIMORE GitHub organization.

title: Software & Resources
text:
Calibration Method for Multi-Channel Sensor
Repository intended to contain code related to work on flexible calibration methods for low-cost photometric and radiometric measurements.
Open repository →

title: Software & Resources
text:
ESP32-S3 Camera GUI
Educational PySide6 GUI and ESP32-S3 firmware for Wi-Fi camera streaming, TCP control and high-resolution snapshot capture.
Open repository →

title: Software & Resources
text:
Educational Serial Monitor GUI
Educational PySide6 desktop GUI for serial device control, live analog signal plotting, peak detection, data inspection and Arduino/ESP32 firmware examples.
Open repository →

title: Software & Resources
text:
Responsible reuse
Each repository is the authoritative source for its requirements, setup instructions and current status. Review its README and license information before reuse.
